description: Add a versioned workflow progress schema and unified TUI projection so workflow runs expose phase, step, agent, round, and waiting state clearly while remaining read-only and safe on partial metadata.

userStories.title: Define workflow rendering schema v1
userStories.description: As a workflow author, I want one versioned progress schema so that built-in and external workflows can target the same TUI contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a schema definition with explicit `version`, `name`, `status`, and `phase` fields
Define optional schema sections for `round`, `steps`, `agents`, and `history`
Document required fields, optional fields, and fallback behavior for missing sections
Built-in and external workflows use the same schema module or type definition
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add unified workflow progress projection
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one read model for workflow progress so that the TUI renders from a single projection instead of transcript parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a projection that stores header, step, agent, round, and wait-state data for the active workflow run
Projection updates incrementally from workflow execution state and task state
Projection is the render source for workflow header, timeline, agent summary, and round history
TUI workflow rendering no longer reconstructs state from raw transcript text on each paint
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add fallback mapping for partial workflow metadata
userStories.description: As a user, I want older or simpler workflows to render safely so that missing metadata does not break the workflow view.
acceptanceCriteria: Projection accepts minimal status-only progress updates without throwing
Missing round data renders `Round unknown`
Missing step, agent, or history data is omitted or replaced with stable fallback labels
Fallback mapping distinguishes `running`, `waiting`, `failed`, and `done` states
Unknown schema fields are ignored without crashing the TUI
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Emit minimal schema payload from workflow runtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I want active workflow runs to publish normalized progress metadata so that the TUI can render deterministic state.
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow runtime emits schema v1 payloads with workflow name, status, phase, and start time when a run starts
Round-based workflows emit current round and total rounds when those values are known
Waiting-for-user, waiting-for-agents, and retrying states emit distinct status and reason fields
Built-in workflows without richer metadata still produce a minimal payload through the fallback path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render workflow progress header
userStories.description: As a user, I want a persistent workflow header so that I can identify what is running and which phase it is in right away.
acceptanceCriteria: Render a dedicated workflow header whenever a workflow run is active
Header shows workflow name, goal or input summary, current status, and start time
Header shows a deterministic phase label from the workflow projection
Header shows current round and max rounds when available, otherwise `Round unknown`
Header renders no pause, resume, cancel, retry, or edit controls
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Render current workflow step timeline
userStories.description: As a user, I want a visible step timeline so that I can tell which workflow step is active, done, blocked, or pending.
acceptanceCriteria: Render a step list or timeline for the current workflow run
Each step shows exactly one normalized state: `pending`, `active`, `completed`, `blocked`, `failed`, or `waiting`
Active step styling is distinct from completed and pending styling
Completed steps remain visible until the workflow run ends
Blocked, failed, or waiting steps show a one-line reason when a reason is available
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Render waiting and retry states clearly
userStories.description: As a user, I want waiting and retry states to be explicit so that I do not mistake idle time for a broken workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: When the projection reports user wait, the TUI shows `waiting for user` and the decision topic
When the projection reports agent wait, the TUI shows `waiting for agents` and the related step or round
When the projection reports retrying, the TUI shows a retrying state and the retried step label
Waiting-state styling is visually distinct from failed-state styling
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Project per-agent activity metadata
userStories.description: As a developer, I want agent activity captured in the workflow projection so that the UI can show what each agent has done without transcript synthesis.
acceptanceCriteria: Projection stores one entry per participating agent with name or role, state, summary, and last-updated time
Projection marks active agents and agents that completed work in the current round from structured metadata
Agents with no activity expose the fallback state `not started`
Projection allows early-round summaries to contain questions, hypotheses, or next decisions without rewriting the text
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render per-agent activity summary
userStories.description: As a user, I want a compact per-agent summary so that I can understand contributions without reading full transcripts.
acceptanceCriteria: Render one row or card per participating agent for the current workflow run
Each agent entry shows name or role, current state, latest action summary, and last updated time
Active agents are visually marked as active
Agents that completed work in the current round are visibly marked for that round
Agents with no activity render the label `not started`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Project compact round history data
userStories.description: As a developer, I want round summaries stored in the workflow projection so that the UI can show how the workflow changed over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Projection stores completed rounds with round number, status, participating agents, and a short summary
Projection keeps the current round separately addressable from completed rounds
Round summaries update incrementally as rounds complete
Projection retains enough data to answer which agents contributed in each recorded round
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render compact round history
userStories.description: As a user, I want a compact round history so that I can review completed rounds without losing sight of the current work.
acceptanceCriteria: Render a history section for completed rounds in the current workflow run
Current round is expanded by default
Older completed rounds are collapsed or compacted by default
Each round entry shows round number, status, participating agents, and a short summary
History layout keeps the current round, active step, and active agents visible in standard terminal width
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Document external schema adoption and phased rollout
userStories.description: As a product and platform team, we want clear compatibility and rollout guidance so that workflows can adopt the schema incrementally without custom TUI branches.
acceptanceCriteria: Document schema v1 field definitions with example payloads for minimal and enriched workflow updates
Document compatibility rules for older, newer, and partial schema payloads
Document Phase 1 as header plus active-step visibility plus waiting-state clarity
Document Phase 2 as per-agent activity summary
Document Phase 3 as compact round history and richer schema adoption
Document that each phase can ship independently without requiring every workflow to emit the richest metadata
Typecheck passes
